text:
10 MARCH 1958

I. THE COMMUNIST BLOC
Moscow propaganda on SEATO conference extends nuclear-free zone proposal to Southeast Asia.
USSR continues to negotiate for additional arms shipments to Near East.
Belgrade breaks ideological truce with Moscow by attacking Marshal Konev.

II. ASIA- AFRICA
Indonesian Government asks for immediate evacuation of Americans from certain areas

text:
Central Sumatra as military campaign apparently gets under way.
Egypt and Syria continue to emphasize charges that King Saud plotted assassination of Nasir and overthrow of UAR. Saud offers to meet with Nasir to refute charges.

III. THE WEST
French Embassy in Rabat fears Spain may take steps that would lead to conflict with Royal Moroccan Army, and that in any case Morocco may take issue to UN.
Cuba — Batista accepts offer by church-sponsored "Committee of National Harmony" to mediate between government and opposition

text:
USSR Continues to Supply Arms to Middle East

The USSR continues to equip Middle East forces with bloc arms. During Yemeni Crown Prince Badr’s visit to Moscow in January, the USSR agreed to provide Yemen with $3,750,000 for the purchase of bloc weapons. The bloc completed military deliveries in September 1957 under the earlier $10,000,000 contract with Yemen.

Egypt is requesting the USSR to provide 20 more transport aircraft—15 of which are to be suitable for paratroop operations—to meet its air force requirements in 1958. The USSR presumably will supply the aircraft, since it agreed in early 1957 to meet Egypt’s military requirements for reequipment and development

text:
Repercussions of Alleged Saudi Plot Against Nasir

Cairo and Damascus continue to elaborate on their charges that King Saud plotted to overthrow the UAR and assassinate Nasir. Saud is also being charged with financing a monarchist plot against the Egyptian regime, and a witness at the current trial of the Egyptians implicated said "There is no bigger fool than Saud." The Egyptians have launched a press campaign designed to split Saudi ranks by claiming that Saud sold out to the United States in the Dhahran airfield agreement against the advice of his pro-Egyptian brother, Crown Prince Faysal. Saud has offered to meet Nasir, either in Riyadh or Cairo, in order to refute the charges against him. In Damascus, about a hundred Saudis have demonstrated against Saud.

Egypt has ordered "all subjects of the UAR working in Saudi Arabia" to leave as soon as possible after 8 March. It is unclear whether this affects the many thousands of Egyptians and Syrians employed in the government and in private enterprises. Their withdrawal would probably gravely affect the functioning of the Saudi economy and government. Saud, in retaliation, has ordered the recall of Saudi military and naval students now training in Egypt. He has requested that the US military mission submit plans for taking over their training. The chief of the Saudi UN delegation, Shuqayri, representing Saud at the Geneva Maritime Conference, has informed Nasir of his disgust with Saud and his intention to resign.

In Iraq and Jordan there is widespread belief in the plot charges, but the governments are backing Saud fully. Many informed Arabs believe that the plot charges will result in division of the Arab world into monarchist and republican camps, with the latter growing consistently stronger. Previous reports that Iraqi troops had moved westward toward Jordan late in February appear to be substantiated by a Syrian message which states that two battalions entered Jordan at that time
